title: Vercel Got Hacked: What It Means for Every Next.js Developer (And What to Do Now)
whyTrending: Vercel confirmed a breach on April 19-20, 2026. ShinyHunters selling data for $2M. Environment variables compromised. Every Next.js dev on Vercel needs to rotate secrets NOW. Massive HN discussion.

title: Claude Opus 4.7: What Actually Changed for Developers (Hands-On Review)
whyTrending: Dropped April 16. Hi-res vision, task budgets, new tokenizer (35% more tokens!), adaptive thinking replaces extended thinking. Breaking change: budget_tokens removed. Every Claude Code user is affected.

title: Next.js 16 Migration Guide: Everything That Breaks (And How to Fix It)
whyTrending: Next.js 16 is out with breaking changes: middleware→proxy rename, Turbopack default, async request APIs only, Cache Components. Plus 16.2 already shipped with 400% faster dev server. Time to migrate.

title: TypeScript 7.0 and Project Corsa: The Go Rewrite That Makes TS 10x Faster
whyTrending: TS compiler rewritten in Go. VS Code type-checks in 7.5s instead of 77s. TS 6.0 is the last JS-based version (bridge release). Every TypeScript developer needs to understand what's coming.

title: The Vercel Breach Playbook: How to Audit Your Next.js App's Supply Chain Security
whyTrending: Post-Vercel-breach, supply chain security is top of mind. Context.ai browser extension was the entry point. Practical guide: audit env vars, rotate secrets, review OAuth apps, check build logs.

title: Node.js vs Bun vs Deno in 2026: The Runtime War Has a Clear Winner
whyTrending: Node 24 with native TS, Bun 2.0 stable (2-4x faster HTTP), Deno 3.0 with KV storage. WinterCG ensures API compatibility. The winner depends on your use case — honest comparison with benchmarks.

title: AI Agents Are Not Replacing Developers — CNN, METR, and the Data Behind the Headlines
whyTrending: CNN April 8: 'The demise of software engineering jobs has been greatly exaggerated.' Pragmatic Engineer newsletter on AI impact. Counter-narrative to the doom takes — with actual data.

title: The Claude Code Source Leak: What We Learned from the NPM Map File
whyTrending: Claude Code source leaked via npm source map. HN went wild. Internal mechanisms revealed: frustration regexes, undercover mode, message filtering. What it means for trust and transparency.

title: React Server Components + Cache Components: The Caching Model Next.js 16 Actually Gets Right
whyTrending: Next.js 16 replaces implicit caching with opt-in 'use cache' directive. Cache Components make caching explicit and composable. Fixes the biggest complaint about Next.js 15's caching behavior.

title: Internal Developer Platforms in 2026: Why 'Build Tools' Became 'Build Platforms'
whyTrending: IDP is the new DevOps. Self-service portals with pre-approved infra and tooling. Backstage, Port, Cortex — the shift from individual tools to platform engineering.
